task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Crop Volume module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Crop Volume' module panel is visible in the module selection area or sidebar.

task_completion.description: Region of Interest (ROI) is created and visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored bounding box (ROI) is visible in the 2D slice views (Red, Yellow, or Green windows) or the 3D view.

task_completion.description: ROI is adjusted to tightly enclose the brain
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ROI bounding box in the slice views is resized to surround only the brain tissue, excluding the large black background areas.

task_completion.description: Cropped volume is generated via the Apply button
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Apply' button is clicked, or a new volume entry (distinct from the original MRHead) appears in the Data/Subject Hierarchy list.

task_completion.description: Save dialog is configured with correct filename and path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' or 'Export' dialog is open. Filename is set to 'MRHead_cropped.nrrd' and directory is '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/' (or similar visual path).

task_completion.description: Final file exists or volume dimensions are verified reduced
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window shows 'MRHead_cropped.nrrd' in the target folder, OR the 'Volumes' module information panel shows the new volume has dimensions smaller than 256x256x130.

integrity.description: Agent used the GUI rather than terminal commands for cropping
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console or system terminal is not used to execute 'slicer.modules.cropvolume' commands directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual 3D Slicer interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual 3D Slicer application window with 3D/slice views, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually create a dummy file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use 'touch' or a text editor to create a file named 'MRHead_cropped.nrrd' without performing the actual cropping operation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Original MRHead dimensions are 256x256x130. The ROI appears as a colored box in slice views. A correct crop results in dimensions smaller than the original. The output filename must be 'MRHead_cropped.nrrd'.
